page: Amazon Web Services r8idn.48xlarge is a memory-optimized, network-optimized, and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical Intel cores with a dedicated allocation running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, which is designed to support memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

For local storage, the r8idn.48xlarge includes 11400 GB of high-speed NVMe SSD instance store volumes. This local storage capacity is bundled directly with the instance, offering low-latency read and write operations. The network architecture delivers a baseline bandwidth of 300 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfer and high-throughput communication across distributed environments.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile represents a high-density resource allocation where massive memory capacity, high-speed local NVMe storage, and extreme network bandwidth are consolidated into a single physical host. This consolidation reduces the need for multiple smaller instances, optimizing resource density for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, and high-performance computing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idn.48xlarge is a memory-optimized, network-optimized, and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical Intel cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of system memory. The instance is equipped with 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. This hardware profile is engineered for high-density, memory-intensive workloads that require low-latency local storage and substantial network throughput. It is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, real-time analytics, and enterprise-grade data processing applications. The high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core and dedicated CPU allocation ensure consistent performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
